speaker: MATT
raw_text: Roll for attack, go for it. As Jester releases her grip, this giant, heavy-looking but gorgeous glittering lollipop apparates out of the air, streaking down towards the goblin's face. Not the goblin, the ogre's-- giant's face. It's been a week.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja. I am leaning, crouching against that stone, hands on the wall, just listening and working on keeping my gourd from rising.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, good to know. Jester, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you so badly want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I want to so bad. I want to use my Spiritual Weapon!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then use it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, well, I will. I'm going to hold my symbol in my hand and do like this because I've seen Caleb do fancy things, and then I'm going to make it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Where would you like to place it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right above his face!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (counting) 30 is where you can make it appear.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Really close to his face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let me see what the range is on it. What's the range on the spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking. 60!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 60! Yeah, you can do it, right over.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Directly in front of his face, flat side facing him, and then just poof!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That ends your turn. It's now the giant's turn. As Yasha has just torn into the side, it's going to turn back to you and is going to double club attack you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! That hits. Then 1d8 plus four. That's cocked. Yeah! 12 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As it hits, (impact) (pained groan) He's shaking the hand as it's going numb. The one eye is half shut. Going (pained groans).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: While he's shaking it off, I'm going to use my bonus action to Toll the Dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You mean your action. Because you already used your bonus action.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use my action to Toll the Dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was thinking about you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's going to have to roll a wisdom of 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Wisdom of 14. Natural 19, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (farcical Czech accent) Two wild and crazy clerics.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Bullshit. So he saw me. I'm going to jump down behind, north side. Northwest side, right in the corner there. I'm going to go (small squeak) and try to not get hit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Well, healing potion isn't hit dice. Hit dice is only when you're taking a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have a whole minute?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb says. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Potentially, guys, here, figure out what your plan is until someone touches it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can heal Molly while that's going on, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to give Molly a healing potion.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He doesn't roll to wake up or anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, he doesn't. He stabilizes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to move back by where Fjord is right now, behind that column.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Me and you take the head, maybe we can--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Moving which one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stand me by Fjord, to the north.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take a healing potion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 2d4, I think I roll it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does she roll the healing potion if I take it, or do I roll it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't matter who rolls it, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, because I was curious if my locket gives me-- does it count as my hit dice or no?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been a number of rounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take my bonus action and Three Stooges style, go poke, right in his eye. That's a natural 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You still get advantage because he's prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, let me see if I get that crit. No, 18 though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn it, that would be great. Crit eye poke!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: God, a one again. Five damage. Lot of crappy damage rolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Poof, right in the eye. (roaring) The eye closes instinctually, and the arms begin to flail, as it's trying to--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we allowed to attack, or no?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, because that's the end of Beau's turn, now it's Fjord's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the end of Beau's turn, it's going to go ahead and use its recovered legendary action to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you run away before he can get an opportunity of attack on you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Technically he hasn't had a-- oh, no, he did, because it's a whole round back, never mind.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There are copper flung from the audience, from the row. You guys take that moment. While it's frustrating to share it, maybe, you made it. One of the gates on the opposite side opens up and a group of seven individuals begin to approach in various robes. You recognize a lot of them as people that were in the lounge earlier before you entered this actual arena. They're carrying a small chest of iron and dark set wood and as they place it on the ground, they begin to pull out sacks of money. They're presenting you the divided prize. That's three rounds for the group and each round was 200 gold. That's 600 gold that you guys get to divide and then the final pot was 2000 divided amongst the two teams. An additional 1000 to your team. So 1600 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Throw some money instead!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's past dusk now. At this point, nine o'clock in the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can't believe I find myself agreeing with Nott here. We should get out of town while it's still possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Really? We're just starting to climb our way up through this thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You leave the table when you've got the pot. You don't wait for everything to go back to the dealer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think for a day or two, everyone here is going to be very preoccupied in the city. If there is anything you are thinking of doing, it would be a good time to do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should steal everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: At some point, they're going to start looking for people to fight this war, and I'm not sure I want to be around for it, because boy they know who we are now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What I am suggesting is a compromise. Not leaving immediately, but soon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I need time to go back to the Pillow Trove, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: DM, can you remind me what time of day it is?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You overhear the gist of most of the conversation, best you can without catching too much attention to yourself. Over the next 20 minutes or so, you catch what amounts to them discussing possible future difficulty in crossing border boundaries between the empire, the Menagerie Coast, and imports. The necessity of them, but having to make sure they go through very stringent processes in doing so, with the promise of coming conflict. He doesn't outright say that they're at war yet, but the Starosta is essentially laying the groundwork for maintaining trade lines through the Wuyun Gorge, across the Cyrios Mountains. Wanting to make sure that the port towns of the Menagerie Coast still have access to get the necessary goods to the empire, not only to maintain their people, but to get them as quickly as possible to the front to the east.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but you also dropped a horse apple in front of them as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it was kind of great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What'd she do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She mentioned a little insight on the pending war when no one knows about the pending war.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think they're going to look at you as a good resource. I think that's what you revealed yourself as.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Either that, or they think that I'm a Xhorhasian assassin, and I am not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What the fuck is the Kryn though?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Kryn is the kingdom over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Kryn.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's the Kryn dynasty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, the Kryn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kryn is the empress of the drow, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The green is something else.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm not here. I'm next to Starosta.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It didn't devolve into a punching fest. That's pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is the Starosta still here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Starosta is still here, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm still there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was envisioning a big, evil monster.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys are in the process of finishing this up and people are starting to leave from the lounge area and they're starting to close down, Darrow comes up with his troupe-- they're a ways into their cups-- and he's like, "Friends, before we shove off and probably never meet again, I just wanted to say it's been an absolute pleasure to get to meet you, to watch you fight, for you to watch us fight. All the luck in your future endeavors."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Finish the evening off right. I'll honestly say I'd like to get out of here as soon as possible so they don't think we're running from the news. We get out of here before the news hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough. All at once?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where do we go?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't care. I'm just happier moving than staying still.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How quickly are you thinking?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If it were just the two of us, I'd say leave tonight, but--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How about 24 hours from now?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: See if we can make it faster, but 24 hours is not terrifying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Dusk tomorrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No later than dusk. I would like to get out before news starts to spread. Before they decide to start locking down the holes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. One more day. Just to get supplies and get ready for the road again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a fair point.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we divide up the money?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I believe that's 228 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Northeast-ish. East of Rexxentrum. That's where we've been stationed for the past six or seven months or so. Done a few jobs here and there."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we divide up the money?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I believe that's 228 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys are in the process of finishing this up and people are starting to leave from the lounge area and they're starting to close down, Darrow comes up with his troupe-- they're a ways into their cups-- and he's like, "Friends, before we shove off and probably never meet again, I just wanted to say it's been an absolute pleasure to get to meet you, to watch you fight, for you to watch us fight. All the luck in your future endeavors."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Same to you. It was very impressive. Where does fate take you after this night?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, as far as I know, we're probably heading back up to Nogvurot and continuing with our contract of sitting around and being paid for nothing."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that-- remind me again-- north of here? Northwest?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Not privately, no. Like I said, I've only been here for about a little over a half a year. To me, it's not been too bad."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. Legal trouble, or illegal trouble?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What you will.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you're going to go illegal, you can actually go just beyond the boundaries of the Empire to Shadycreek Run. Entire haven for murderers, thieves, and all sorts of strange people."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe if it's not so gnarly?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is Deastok to the west, as well as Kamordah. There's quite good drink in Kamordah. There's Pride's Call if you like the jovial dwarvenfolk there. Much less dark and intense as Grimgolir, because they're right on the boundary and most of them are fighting for the war as well. Personally, Rexxentrum's got it all."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We hear that. Soltryce Academy is located there as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, that it is."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do you know anybody that's graduated from that academy?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Maybe in a week. Give me some time. A lot of stuff percolating right now, but Leaky Tap, right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right, right. Yes-- "

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My friends call me Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Fair enough. What can I do for you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I couldn't help but notice your body posture, for this whole party, has been rather tense. I don't know if you noticed, but I am a bit of a healer, and I'm from the Menagerie Coast, where I did a lot of mental healing, as well as body healing. If there's anything you need to relieve your stress, I'm very good at this. This is why I came here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He gives a look and goes, "Uh," and looks back and forth between his companions. He goes, "What are you insinuating?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm talking about somebody to talk to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right! Right."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: To relieve your mind of your stress!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Right. I really appreciate that offer. Genuinely, Jester Fancypants McGee. However, such is my life-- "

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know you have people that work for you--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Since you've been in town? It's probably been a week and a half to two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I am a healer! I'm a really good healer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you tell him that he has to give you a dollar to engage your services?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, I told him-- I didn't give him a price, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, that's even shiftier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That means I'm really good, is what that means.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You charge for these services?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not for friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) I'm actually really shit at giving advice, but don't tell anyone, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't agree. I feel like you would be very good at it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, any time you have any problems, Yasha, you just come to me, okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'm maybe showing a little bit of pink on my cheeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we talking about rolling at the Pillow Trove this evening? Is that what I am picking up on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I need to go by there anyway. How long has it been since we've been in town?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Jester. Hold on just a second." She gets up and walks out of the room. A minute passes. Two minutes pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's it?! Four gold! Do you know how much she wanted to charge me before? I'm going to leave it though, because I am walking away. I'm at the front desk.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Four gold for two rooms?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For each room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll cover the fellas' room. Here's four gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Here's four more for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This was her ceding to the Victory Pit victory and return customer price. Jester, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to see if my package is here from my mom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you head to where you went previously, which is a little side chamber. There's a young woman there waiting, who is falling asleep at the desk. "Oh, I'm sorry! I didn't see you there. Can I help you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I blend in with my environment very often. It's common.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. Well, very well done."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have a package here, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Jester.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About five minutes pass before she enters with a small parcel under her arm, and goes, "I believe this is to you, Jester, correct?" It's a small box about that big, it says "Jester" written in nice handwriting on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to see if my package is here from my mom.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you head to where you went previously, which is a little side chamber. There's a young woman there waiting, who is falling asleep at the desk. "Oh, I'm sorry! I didn't see you there. Can I help you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I blend in with my environment very often. It's common.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. Well, very well done."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have a package here, probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Jester. Hold on just a second." She gets up and walks out of the room. A minute passes. Two minutes pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Anything on the desk of value?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On the desk of this woman who has left? There looks to be a small oil lantern set there that's flickering. It's well-made.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nice pen or anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it looks like she took it with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I keep waiting.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No. First things first. The skies are gray. What little bits of sunlight came through the last day have completely absconded. The sky is covered in gray, nearly stormy clouds. There is an air of stress and question in the streets as you start seeing Righteous Brand groups making their way through. As you make your way through the north side of the Pentamarket, one of the town criers comes by shouting, "War declared on the Kryn by the King in retribution of attack on eastern garrisons!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Where are y'all going?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We have to take a trip to Pumat's for supplies and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Insight check! Just kidding. I'm good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Who's going where?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go wherever Caleb goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pumat, library.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pumat, library. The rest of you guys are going to Dolan's?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To Dolan's. We'll meet up at Pumat's.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That sounds good. You guys, Dolan's; we'll go library.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll do library first. Then we'll pull Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll pull Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can pull Matt's soul.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pull Matt Key.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. As you guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (silly voice) Hey, you got your chocolate in my peanut butter!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys exit the Pillow Trove, and you make your way outside of the Tri-Spires, the first thing of note is the tone and the mood of the city has shifted dramatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a more festive atmosphere?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a party, girl.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Another one, a few moments later further down the way shouts out, "Righteous Brand mobilizing east to protect our families! Spare what provisions you can to ensure victory and support our defenders!" There are clusters of Righteous Brand groups, the full armored military outside of the Signet Wall that have come into the streets and are basically picking up provisions, asking for donations from anyone that can spare things for this scenario. They're grabbing horses from nearby stables, and it seems like since the close of the festival, through the night and into the morning, preparations have already kicked in on the military side.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (silly voice) Hey, you got your chocolate in my peanut butter!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys exit the Pillow Trove, and you make your way outside of the Tri-Spires, the first thing of note is the tone and the mood of the city has shifted dramatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a more festive atmosphere?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a party, girl.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. First things first. The skies are gray. What little bits of sunlight came through the last day have completely absconded. The sky is covered in gray, nearly stormy clouds. There is an air of stress and question in the streets as you start seeing Righteous Brand groups making their way through. As you make your way through the north side of the Pentamarket, one of the town criers comes by shouting, "War declared on the Kryn by the King in retribution of attack on eastern garrisons!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Another one, a few moments later further down the way shouts out, "Righteous Brand mobilizing east to protect our families! Spare what provisions you can to ensure victory and support our defenders!" There are clusters of Righteous Brand groups, the full armored military outside of the Signet Wall that have come into the streets and are basically picking up provisions, asking for donations from anyone that can spare things for this scenario. They're grabbing horses from nearby stables, and it seems like since the close of the festival, through the night and into the morning, preparations have already kicked in on the military side.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (silly voice) Hey, you got your chocolate in my peanut butter!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys exit the Pillow Trove, and you make your way outside of the Tri-Spires, the first thing of note is the tone and the mood of the city has shifted dramatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a more festive atmosphere?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a party, girl.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. First things first. The skies are gray. What little bits of sunlight came through the last day have completely absconded. The sky is covered in gray, nearly stormy clouds. There is an air of stress and question in the streets as you start seeing Righteous Brand groups making their way through. As you make your way through the north side of the Pentamarket, one of the town criers comes by shouting, "War declared on the Kryn by the King in retribution of attack on eastern garrisons!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I see you are aware of the shifts in our current imperial state of affairs. We haven't had war in generations. I'm hopeful that the conflict is swift, but I'll leave that concern to those with the right expertise. I'm tasked with keeping my people safe here, not on the front. Here. Now, wartime puts us in an unfortunate scenario. Zadash effectively governs the southern half of the empire and most of military might is now leaving us. Our Crownsguard will be stretched thin across keeping the peace and dealing with unrest and worry and protecting these walls from whatever pushes through the lines of battle and the general things that wander from beyond our boundaries. Money isn't an issue, but personnel is. There are outlying threats and criminals we no longer have the manpower for and as such, the Crown has approved an expanded mercenary budget. Now you've already proven your ability to me twice. Most recent, your skills in the Victory Pit showing you capable and loyal to the king. I know not your interest, but if you would come under contact, under my office as legionnaires of the empire, you would be responsible for protecting the lives of many innocent people. Now, under contract you would receive 15 gold a week per person as a stipend whether or not you were on contract or not. It could be at your leisure, months at a time with no work and you still get paid no matter what you do. Each assignment rewards its own bounty of course, case by case. The work I'll be presenting would largely involve the hunting of outstanding criminals, the acquisition of resources that may benefit the Empire in this time of war, and sometimes the extermination of dangerous creatures that threaten life away from the front lines. Now you'd all have credentials within the Empire and that will grant you access to supplies, pro bono. Access to empirical halls, and individuals otherwise restricted to the general populace."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, we were right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's fantastic. Please, have a seat. I'd like to discuss a few things with you if you don't mind. First off, congratulations yesterday. That was quite a display and a fantastic victory. Well--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we going to war?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few tomes are retrieved. "This here is a recollection of what elements of historical significance could be pulled from the remnants of the Age of Arcanum, pre-Calamity. There are a great number of mages out there, though many of them befell darker interests. But here are those books. If you're looking for more modern mages, we have historical information on the past 200 years of empirical magecraft and powerful wizards in that realm."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Memberships.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are led to a section. Madeer reads through. "Arcanists, you say? Historically? Are we talking Age of Arcanum?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am looking for histories, accounts of people who pushed the boundaries of what can be done over the ages. The firsts. I am interested in more than what is typical, at least as much as can be known. Do you follow me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I believe so."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's a magic fan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, that is true, I am a magic fan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Little heavy-handed, man. Like, really heavy-handed.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You're a speed reader, but this is also very dense material, and it is essentially a historical theorizing on historical elements they've been able to piece together. I'd say by the time you've gotten here, it'd be about noon in the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That would be more Soltryce Academy material. They do lay claim to most of those theoretical discussions and research. The Cerberus Assembly would be the keepers of those research endeavors."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Excellent, I shall go there next.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have a book here on the Cerberus Assembly, if that would be of interest to you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These tomes are presented to you. You can spend time to read if you'd like. Madeer watches the entire time, like a hawk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start reading.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which book do you go through first?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I will start with the first, the oldest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Are you absorbing it, or are you skimming through?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This one, I am absorbing it. I will read it cover to cover.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cover to cover will take you the better part of the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm a speed reader, and an average novel takes an hour to an hour and a half for a speed reader to read.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You read through that tome. It's about three in the afternoon now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I spend all my time with that book.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Madeer. How's it been? How's it going?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It has been frustrating, but fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking Zeenoth, am I right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth is a great friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, he's a great friend! Great guy. Great guy, that Zeenoth. Has Dairon left?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have not seen her this morning, but I don't know if she's left or not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool, cool.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It has a breakdown of previous members who passed away. It's basically a historical dialogue of how it was established. The name was actually a reference to a group that existed in the Age of Arcanum. It talks about how there was a war of mage houses 150 years ago within the empire that nearly destroyed part of the capital, Rexxentrum, and as part of the ceasefire and the arrest of these mage houses that were at war, as opposed to imprisoning them, they agreed to bind themselves to the empire and work for them. That's what the Cerberus Assembly was established as: a power under rule of the king. Since then has flourished. It's helped the kingdom rise to power, it helped them conquer the Julous Dominion. They work with and under the king. It talks of all the members and it talks about what they do. Trent is in there. There is Master Trent Ikithon, who is the Archmage of Civil Influence. Civil Influence is the title and it talks about how he is the propagandist and the constructor of the face of the Empire in upper-echelon circles. It speaks of Martinet Lunidus Da'leth who is the Archmage of Domestic Protections, who is the oldest in the original member of the Assembly who still lives. Was there when it was built and is currently considered the leader of the Assembly. It speaks of Marquis Jenna Iresor who is the Archmage of Industry. One of the younger members of the Assembly. There is Master Doolan Tversky who is the Archmage of Dysology.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It has been frustrating, but fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking Zeenoth, am I right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth is a great friend.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, he's a great friend! Great guy. Great guy, that Zeenoth. Has Dairon left?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have not seen her this morning, but I don't know if she's left or not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool, cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You read through that tome. It's about three in the afternoon now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Caleb, not going to conquer the world in a library visit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you give me two more hours?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I cannot give you two more hours. I can't. I will gouge my eyes out with a fucking pencil if I give you two more hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How about one more hour? We'll all compromise.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What about 30 more minutes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you have something in your neverending flask?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Of course, would you like a drink? Are we allowed to drink in here? Why are you carrying around a cup?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I skim the book on the Cerberus Assembly.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys, what have you been up to during this time? Because it's been a number of hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I will say that I got my fill. I can feel this one's radiating--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, burrowing vision into your neck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I want to stay for 17 more hours, but I'm ready to go. I am good. I am done. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course! Good day."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thanks, Madeer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good to see you, Beauregard." He escorts you out of the Archive.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb looks at the thousands of books that he did not read and leaves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and there's a whole second level above the spiral staircase.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we count our fucking blessings.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, you're not going to find any answers in any of those books.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go find the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We've already left the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We are in Rexxentrum.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're in a different campaign.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean it's been 17 years, Pumat? What do you mean?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Fair enough." About five minutes pass and then the Pumat comes down, he's got a small, bound cluster of bolts and he goes, "Hey, so it's all we got, but we've got a batch here of ten enchanted crossbow bolts. These upon firing should offer a small batch of fiery impact on whatever you strike with them."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what does it do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No armor, not even light armor?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you wear armor, Yasha?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Beauregard does not either.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How much are those?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That one will run you back about 1,200 gold."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe not, forget that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We'll come back for another day.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have any enchanted crossbow bolts?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let me go ahead and check there for a second, those are generally smaller-scale here. Pumat III, if you don't mind." "I got it, already listening to you, don't worry." He goes up a small ladder in the far corner into an attic place and you hear some rustling around as the other Pumats are there patiently watching. "So yeah, crazy war times, huh?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How much for that shield?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, this here enchanted shield will run you about 550 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think I'm all right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: In the interest of time, Jasna Bree, who is a rather eccentric woman with wild black hair, a slightly hard to follow disposition, greets you and is like, "Hi! Welcome! Glad you could make it! Healing potions, yes. Unfortunately kind of already cleared out. I'm so sorry. Give me a week, I will have a fresh batch of healing potions, and I'd be happy to help."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think we make a good team. I think there's more to be had here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It might run out, who knows. I'm not an optimist, but think there's still more to be had.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do we make a polite exit at the least or do we just bugger out?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She seemed pretty heartfelt, I think a polite exit would be thoughtful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm fair for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Merchants' guild then polite exit?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, maybe we'll rustle up W.C., Loo, John, Crapper, Shitter, and Toilet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh that's right, our horsies!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Any other business you want to do before you decide to leave the city?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did you want to swing by and see if-- Yeah, can we pop into the other shop? The old Grace of the Wild and see if they have any healing potions, just to check out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just in case.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In the interest of time...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do they have any?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You gather the knowledge of this war has blindsided most of the merchant guilds in Zadash. Best you can tell, most of them are in the process of getting ready to figure out what their next endeavor is. Those that would be leaving won't be leaving for a few days. If you hang around, you may be able to pick up that kind of work.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ointments.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I ask a question?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: With a healer's kit, can you heal people when you're not in combat? Or is it just for stabilizing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The healer's kit usually can be used to stabilize somebody. There's a feat you can take that allows you to spend an action to heal someone with it in combat. It's called the Healer feat. Which is actually pretty cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. I'll look at that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It turns anybody into a combat healer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go say goodbye to somebody and leave?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are we checking the merchants' to see if there's any armed escort requests out of the merchants' guild?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Who's helming that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fjord!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm coming with her.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll help you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Us three. Oh. That was bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's helping you with it. It's advantage on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes! (laughs) Oh no! Slightly better. Five.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No. He actually was a legionnaire for us about five years back, but then went rogue, and we believe had ties with the Myriad and became a powerful tent-pole here in the Marrow Valley. We had him cornered in Deastok about three months ago by a team of crownsguard under the leadership of Carlos Ventuga. But this Hawker slew Carlos and fled south into the Wuyun Gorge and disappeared into the Menagerie Coast. We're having to send whoever we can to try and retrieve him and bring him back alive."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think we are probably not going to be able to help out right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I understand."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's not you. It's us. We're not ready for that kind of commitment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I understand, that's all right."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're going to make an adventuring party very happy some day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You really will.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I hope so. We'll send someone else after the Hawker."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: After the what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Hawker."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's a Hawker?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Don't worry about it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Does anyone know what a Hawker is?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Which spelling are you using for that word? A-W-K? O-C-K?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: H-A-W-K.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like a monster or something?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We sent a troupe after him about eight weeks ago, and I fear they may not be coming back at this point. We intended to send some of our top investigators from the capital after this bounty--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: From the Menagerie Coast?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The opposite direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The Menagerie Coast is really freaking cool, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So many choices!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, that sounds really cool!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you need more time, that's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you guys think? Hold on, can we talk about this?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Quick huddle.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pros and cons.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm happy just to leave the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pros, we get to visit the Menagerie Coast, which is always really cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pro, 10,000 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do we have a time limit on when we'd be able to get him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, but the longer it takes, the easier he probably has vanishing into the south."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Makes sense.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's late afternoon at this point. You guys are coming back right as you met with Kara there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit. I'm unstoppable!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's part of the reason that Vax survived so much in the last campaign.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think I get Revivify now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I use that once a day? Once a round?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's just your reaction, once a round.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Once a round?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit. I'm a golden god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I get a new blood curse. I want to have a conversation about that before we go into that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you do get another blood curse, you get to choose.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have Dispel Magic you guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So does Yasha, no big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, that's a big deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you get, of course, 3rd-level spells, and you get another invocation. You can mess with it later.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where we're leaving off, are we going to come back? Because it's the end of the day, are we going to come back at the end of the day or are we going to come back next morning?
